reviews.review_msg: Only share the objective news views in class, never taught how to write a good business writing. Rubric is not clear and the group projects are totally not useful for the future upper level BUS Studies.

It felt like many of the lectures consisted of just Ilia conversing with a rotation of the same few students. AI was heavily encouraged in this class, but using it to your advantage was not taught. The structure of the class doesn't really teach you how to think critically, but instead, how to depend on AI for higher grades.

Ilia was pretty good overall, he's strict but pretty nice and understanding if you talk to him. The TA, Olessya, was the worst TA I've had. Her grading was ridiculously hard, she gave no meaningful feedback, and she would talk the whole time through our timed tutorial assignments, which were hard enough without the distraction and pressure.

Ilia himself is a very nice person and will explain concepts you don't understand if you ask him nicely. the TAs are the WORST enemy because your entire grade depends on them. Olessya A was the worst TA marker and marks like you ate her dog or something. Was 1 percent from the next letter grade so that explains a lot

Ilia himself is a bit intimidating, but he's actually very nice/approachable in office hours. He's not overly accessible outside of class though. I hated this class, and much of that is due to how the 217 is designed. I don't think anything we learned in class was helpful for assignments. Also beware that everything is about AI.

Although many people say he can be intimidating and "hates every sole on Earth" I think he takes his profession seriously and is overall a great teacher. I had lots of fun in his class and I found it challenging enough for me to use my brain and apply concepts that are taught even though AI use was allowed generously.

Ilia had a unique class structure. I've taken two classes with him and he would often times make the class read an article or discuss a current event for 20-30 minutes and spend another 5-15 minutes discussing it as a class. Taking up around 45 minutes for one current event discussion. Which only takes people 5 min to discuss.
He was okay.

You get to choose your own group during the first tutorial, so choose wisely. He also adjusts grades based on peer evaluations, so if someone doesn't pull their weight, he takes it seriously. He provides helpful, actionable feedback during office hours and genuinely supports students who put in the effort.

Take this course with any other prof. Super difficult assignment that he does not teach you how to do at ALL, this course forces you to learn everything you have to do outside the course, while he talks about his perspective on current events the whole time.

He is a good professor. His lectures and interesting and pretty relevent to the course. However, his assignments are quite difficult and are often on the stop and timed. He has clear expectations but they are very high.

Ilia's class is hard because the curve is very tight. The outline is a bit weird but you can see Ilia is a smart prof and is knowledgeable about the economy. He should teach economics, it would suit his character better. Goodluck on your second try. lmfao

Worst prof in beedie so far.

- All he does is talk about global news rather than teaching how to write better.
- Unnecessary thinking fast presentations
- Reviewed the group assignment with the TA, she said it was good and when she marks it, gives my team a bad mark
- Never take it with him
He's based. Honestly think the 1 star reviews are from really soft people lol
do not take this course with this proof, he has useless lectures, tutorials are all writing assignments and there is no time to prepare for them ahead of time. super hard marking.

Great class. I would take it again. He was amazing. He made the class a lot better than most professors. His WDC assignments are a really great exercise. Don't be shy to ask for help, as he gives very concise feedback.

He doesn't actually teach the material,
 and the class is extremely stressful. 
We were only given about 40 minutes to prepare presentations during class. He was unprofessional and biased toward Russian students, making the class environment very unpleasant.

LOVED ilia for 217. took him in the summer, and he was amazing. There are a couple of group assignments, so be sure to pick a good group. Feedback and instructions weren't really clear on the WDC, but overall he's a really nice and understanding prof if you get to know him :)

Do not take the class with him. Has a bunch of assignments that are like pop quizzes you get no context ahead of time and you go to tutorial and just start writing. The other sections of this class have an advantage because it's taught by another teacher. They get their assignment info a week ahead so they have time to prepare. Please beware!!

Ilia is a very nice prof! His lectures can be quite dry, but they give you time to work on group projects or prepare for WDC assignments that are taken in tutorials. You have less than 50 mins to complete them, and the feedback given was not helpful. You can use chat in this course, but Ilia states that to get an A or B, you need to go beyond chat.

Notoriously difficult class, somehow made worse by Ilia. The only content to learn is in the survival guide. Lectures are just discussions and opinions; I learned nothing. I had challenges with group members, Ilia did not help, and my grade suffered.
Political discussions surrounding Biden, Trump, and Elon were part of every class. Terrible.
I don't know how he could still teach in the university, he should go to teach "Early Education"! Oh sorry I forgot, little kids don't like "In the News!"
I have ever seen such a bad instructor! Always to talk useless things (even not know how to do well in this course, read slides and in the news, in the news....
Ended with 74% but got downward curved to a C because of the rank based system.

This was a very annoying class in general. I did well on the individual assignments but my group dragged my mark through the mud. He never taught anything about writing in class and then expected us to do things like write 2 whole pages cited with 3 different sources in less than 50 mins. Also the TA docked points for things we were never taught.

Save urself the stress & take this class w/ a different prof. I don't think I've genuinely learned anything in this class…all we've talked about is “business news” (ramble abt Elon). The criteria for each assignment is very vague. The readings are meant for the assignments but they usually end up being useless or the prof doesn't even go over them.

He doesn't care or delve into the outlines for any assignments so you are unsure about what you need to even do to get a good grade. He spends a lot of time talking about random stuff that is unrelated to the course. There is a written exam that is graded pretty harshly to watch for as well.
Business news discussions are useful
Not recommended overall; avoid choosing another instructor for this course. Waste of money; pay more efforts and get such a low grade :(

This is the type of class you take for the credit and not because you plan to learn anything. You will not learn anything, honestly your grade is almost entirely reliant on how good the group members you are placed with are. The only time the prof talks is when he speaks about current news stories. 

If this class is not for credit don't take lol

Honestly, he was great. He wants you to give the taste of the real world and how BUS360 will be. All the people giving him a 1, look at their grades, putting all the blame on the TAs and the prof. I'd 100% take him again for BUS217w. He's quite knowledgeable and if you think he writes his own reviews, well all the best lol! Really get a grip guys

If you love Elon Musk, you will have lots to talk about with Ilia. Overall didn't feel like I learnt anything from the instructor, classes were an *open discussion* most of the time. (He loves to talk about himself) TAs are on another level, and grading is extremely tough and punitive, for even some things outside of your control : (

Ilia's class was overall good. The assignments were interesting as you got to apply the material to realistic examples to build your understanding. The coursework is graded hard, but it's consistent. The biggest downside is being stuck with a poor group, where the assignments are worth 50%. I think the group work should be worth less.
Take it with any other prof.

Ilia talks a lot about random things during lectures. BUS 217W in itself is a very weird course; it doesn't really teach you anything. Most classes are spent listening to pointless current event lectures as the try-hard students yap with Ilia for 2 hours. Projects are timed/speed-based with TAs docking marks for every small error.

Trust me when I say it, he was the best prof I've had here in SFU, really wants you to succeed and gives amazing feedback The class is designed in a way that you have to learn the course concepts and use them in real life situations or do the implementations. It's all about how good you are with active learning. It's not him writing this lol.

If you LOVE tesla and elon musk take this class! If you have any other interests in life and love happiness, your well being, and hope - DO NOT! This was the worst class I have ever taken in my university experience, possibly even my entire education career path. Please take 217 with anyone else! Susan Christie Bell is a saint in comparison!!!!!!!!
He is awesome, but the course is not an easy way to get a higher mark! Just choose this section without any doubts!
TA's of the class are such hard markers, you will do so much work just to get a bad work. bro showed up 10 mins late once and did not say nothing. Talks for most of the class. just dont take it w him

A lot of people like him BUT he does not go over anything to do with the course, assignment expectations or teach anything in class really. It's mostly just a yap session for him about current events. He only gives brief guidelines AFTER the assignments on how to improve. Idk what I learned...just to type fast.

If you enjoy keeping up to date with economics or politics, this is the class for you. Professor Bykov allows an environment for open dialogue on a broad range of subjects, which is complemented nicely by his knowledge on a vast array of topics. Despite the lower grade, this has been my favourite class (and professor) in university thus far.

Ilia's course has quickly become one of my all-time favorites! The lectures are filled with practical and useful content. I loved the In The News segment at the beginning of each class. The assignments are manageable if you read the material beforehand. The grading system is fair, the feedback provided is constructive
Amazing prof. With an engaging teaching style and deep industry knowledge, they create an enriching learning environment that makes complex business concepts accessible and enjoyable.

Already a poor course to begin with. David makes the experience even worse. Lectures and assignments are thinly linked. Deadlines are quick, some assignments are due the very same day at the end of class. Marking is slow. He's very out of touch with students, asking him questions will get you long winded non-answers.

Horrible prof. Hes always talking about his UBC classes and always putting last minute effort to our class. Groups are supposed to switch 3-4 times (this is reflected in ALL 217 classes). We switched twice. Assignments are also made together for all sections yet ours was entirely different and instructions were so vague. AVOID AT ALL COSTS

I am so fortunate to have studied under Darelle during my 1st semester at Beedie. I looked forward to attending this class each week & I credit that to her. In a time of gloom, she brightened up my day. She was the highlight of my week. Darelle is everything you want in an SFU instructor. I am grateful for the time spent learning/growing with her.

BUS 217W, in general, is a more challenging course, but Darelle makes it enjoyable. She emphasizes improvement, not perfection, and is always there for you. As long as you put in the work, participate in class by asking questions and appreciating your classmates, and seek help from her and your TAs, you will succeed. I hope to take BUS 360W w/ her.
She is terrible. Take 217 with someone else. Marks unfairly. Grading criteria is vague. Annoying personality.
Darelle is a super nice person! She is open to our feedback and very supportive. There are a lot of assignments, but that's BUS217.

Hands down the most unfair prof I have ever experienced. Terrible explanation for assignments and the marking is so tough without having the prior knowledge to avoid the strict marking. Class average was very low all semester and she seemed okay with it because it is curved. Dont understand how that is okay. We want to do well not just get by...

She sucks! I would not recommend taking a class with her. She grades unfairly. She nitpicks on everything. She gives out unclear instructions. Her lectures are so boring. She is just dreadful. I hated going to her class. The feedback that she gave was vague and unhelpful. Do yourself a favour and take 217 with someone else.

Brent is one of the nicest and most caring profs I've ever had! He always encourages students to reach out for help and is always there for help if needed. I would definitely recommend taking any course that he teaches because he is an excellent instructor.

Brent is literally the sweetest prof ever. You can tell he really cares about his students and helps them to do better. His OH is very helpful, instructions are clear. I rarely write review for a prof but Brent is the REAL GEM. I almost cried a lil when our class ended LOL. Overall, highly recommended.

Taking 217W with Brent makes you think it is not as difficult as what people usually say. Clear rubric and explanations, detailed feedback, helpful office hours, and instant email responses, Brent is THE GOAT. Work hard and critically think about what is really expected in assignments will guarantee you a more than satisfactory grade!
Brent is a solid choice for BUS 217W, he takes his time and gives constructive feedback on work so you can improve your grade. Also, there is no final exam so that is a plus.

Jane had long, boring lectures. Tough, rigorous marking. Looks for very specific work, attention to detail is an absolute MUST in her class. Lots of group work. People don't usually do well in 217, but I did my best to stand out and put in lots of effort and it paid off for me in Jane's class. Be prepared to go the extra mile to be above the curve.

Jane loves group work. Course was divided into 4 main assignments (15% each) and lots of little quizzes and activities worth less than 10% each. Very long, boring lectures but content was very simple. Class avg was low on every assignment, but gave good feedback for how to improve. Just pay close attention to detail + get all the easy marks.
NOT a good instructor for this course! Could not learn anything from him! Waste of money and time
A very nice prof, choose him without any doubts.
